existing_features.title: Add a 'Feedback' button or link in the main navigation/header (Layout.tsx area, but implement via App.tsx or a new modal/page) that is visible to all authenticated users. This opens a modal form with: a toggle/tab to select 'Request a Feature' or 'Report a Bug'; a Title field; a Description textarea; and a Submit button. On submission, call the backend `submitFeedback` function. Show a success toast on completion and close the modal.

Add a `useFeedbackSubmit` mutation hook that calls `actor.submitFeedback` and invalidates relevant queries on success. Add a `useFeedbackSubmissions` query hook that calls `actor.getFeedbackSubmissions` (used only in the admin view). Add a `useDeleteFeedback` mutation hook that calls `actor.deleteFeedbackSubmission`.

Add a 'Feedback Submissions' section to the AdminPanel component. This section displays all submitted feedback in a table/card list showing: submission type badge (Feature Request / Bug Report), title, description, submitter's short user ID, submitter's full principal, and timestamp. Admins and owner can delete individual submissions. Show loading and empty states appropriately.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add a 'Request a Feature' or 'Report a Bug' form that stores submissions (with user ID) in a list visible to admins and owner, and/or sends an email notification

Add a backend data type `FeedbackSubmission` with fields: id (Nat), submitterPrincipal (Text), submitterUserId (Text), submissionType (variant { FeatureRequest; BugReport }), title (Text), description (Text), timestamp (Int). Store submissions in a stable array or HashMap. Expose the following functions: `submitFeedback(submissionType, title, description)` callable by any authenticated user (captures caller principal and derived user ID automatically); `getFeedbackSubmissions()` callable only by admins and owner, returns all submissions; `deleteFeedbackSubmission(id)` callable only by admins and owner.
